items.quote: Big companies are like marching bands. Even if half the band is playing random notes, it still sounds kind of like music. The concealment of failure is built into them.
items.author: Doug Coupland

items.title: LLM-Oriented Information Retrieval: A Denoising-First Perspective
items.abstract: This paper argues that the primary bottleneck in modern LLM-oriented Information Retrieval (IR) is **denoising**—maximizing usable evidence density due to LLMs' sensitivity to noise and limited attention. The authors conceptualize this shift through a four-stage IR challenge framework and provide a taxonomy of signal-to-noise optimization techniques across the entire retrieval pipeline, from indexing to agentic workflows.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1. Challenge shifts in the history of IR.

items.title: Position: agentic AI orchestration should be Bayes-consistent
items.abstract: This paper argues that while making LLMs themselves explicitly Bayesian is difficult, the **orchestration layer of agentic AI systems should adopt Bayesian principles**. The core contribution is positioning **Bayesian Decision Theory as the necessary framework for coherent decision-making** under uncertainty, enabling the system to maintain and update beliefs about latent quantities to optimally select tools and actions.

items.title: SAGA: Workflow-Atomic Scheduling for AI Agent Inference on GPU Clusters
items.abstract: SAGA addresses the inefficiency of scheduling independent LLM calls for AI agent workflows by introducing **program-level scheduling**, treating the entire multi-step agent task as the fundamental schedulable unit. It uses Agent Execution Graphs to predict and reuse intermediate KV cache states across tool calls, significantly reducing latency. This approach achieves near-optimal scheduling performance while ensuring fairness for complex, chained AI workloads on GPU clusters.

items.title: Silicon Showdown: Performance, Efficiency, and Ecosystem Barriers in Consumer-Grade LLM Inference
items.abstract: This paper systematically analyzes the performance and efficiency trade-offs for running large LLMs (70B+ parameters) on consumer Nvidia and Apple Silicon hardware. For Nvidia, it identifies a "Backend Dichotomy" where the new NVFP4 format boosts throughput significantly but imposes runtime constraints affecting latency. The study also characterizes the "VRAM Wall," forcing users to choose between fitting large models via aggressive quantization or sacrificing model intelligence.

items.title: To Call or Not to Call: A Framework to Assess and Optimize LLM Tool Calling
items.abstract: This paper introduces a decision-making framework to assess and optimize when Large Language Models (LLMs) should utilize external tools, focusing specifically on web search. The framework evaluates tool-use decisions based on necessity, utility, and affordability, using both normative (optimal allocation) and descriptive (observed behavior) perspectives. This approach allows for a principled analysis of LLMs' self-awareness regarding the true benefit of external information retrieval.
items.figure_caption: Given input x x , the model ℳ \( \mathcal{M} \) decides π ​ ( x ) ∈ { 0 , 1 } \( \pi \)(x)\( \in \)\{0,1\} to call a tool (response r r ) or not, producing y = ℳ ​ ( x , r ) y=\( \mathcal{M} \)(x,r) or y = ℳ ​ ( x ) y=\( \mathcal{M} \)(x) . We compare NO TOOL, ALWAYS TOOL, and SELF-DECISION, and evaluate decisions via need (requires help), utility (performance gain), and affordability (cost vs. gain), distinguishing perceived vs. true quantities.

items.title: Evaluating the Architectural Reasoning Capabilities of LLM Provers via the Obfuscated Natural Number Game
items.abstract: This paper introduces the Obfuscated Natural Number Game to evaluate LLMs' **Architectural Reasoning**—the ability to prove theorems using only local axioms in an unfamiliar domain. By renaming identifiers in the Lean 4 formalization, they created a zero-knowledge benchmark. The study finds that while obfuscation universally increases inference time, general models degrade significantly, whereas specialized reasoning models maintain consistent accuracy.
items.figure_caption: LLM performance metrics across varying noise levels \( \lambda \) . The plots illustrate Correct Rate (%) and Average Time (s) for GPT-4o, Claude-Sonnet-4.5, DeepSeek-R1, GPT-5, and DeepSeek-Prover-V2. Error bars represent standard deviation over 5 independent runs.

items.title: RunAgent: Interpreting Natural-Language Plans with Constraint-Guided Execution
items.abstract: RunAgent is a multi-agent platform designed to reliably execute natural-language plans by enforcing determinism through an agentic language with explicit control structures. Its core contribution is integrating natural language understanding with constraint-guided execution, autonomously deriving and validating step-specific constraints. This allows RunAgent to dynamically select appropriate actions (reasoning, tool use, or code execution) while ensuring correctness through verification and error correction.
items.figure_caption: An overview of RunAgent, highlighting its three main modules.

items.title: Stable-GFlowNet: Toward Diverse and Robust LLM Red-Teaming via Contrastive Trajectory Balance
items.abstract: Stable-GFN (S-GFN) addresses the instability and mode collapse issues in using Generative Flow Networks (GFNs) for LLM red-teaming. The core method involves eliminating the partition function ($Z$) estimation via pairwise comparisons and employing robust masking against noisy rewards. This results in more stable training, leading to superior attack performance and greater diversity in identifying LLM vulnerabilities.

items.title: AGoQ: Activation and Gradient Quantization for Memory-Efficient Distributed Training of LLMs
items.abstract: AGoQ introduces a novel quantization method for memory-efficient LLM distributed training by addressing the limitations of standard 4-bit activations and 8-bit gradients. It employs a layer-aware activation quantization to achieve near 4-bit storage and a precision-preserving 8-bit gradient quantization with optimized All-Reduce communication. This approach significantly reduces memory usage (up to 52%) and improves training speed (up to 1.34$\times$) without sacrificing accuracy.
items.figure_caption: An example of Interleaved 1F1B PP with four stages and each mini-batch divided into eight micro-batches.

items.title: Beyond Benchmarks: MathArena as an Evaluation Platform for Mathematics with LLMs
items.abstract: This paper introduces **MathArena** as a continuously maintained evaluation platform designed to overcome the limitations of static benchmarks for assessing LLMs in mathematics. It significantly broadens the original scope to include diverse tasks like proof-based competitions, research-level problems, and formal proof generation in Lean. The core contribution is providing a comprehensive, regularly updated system for reliable comparison and tracking of LLM mathematical reasoning capabilities.

items.title: FinSafetyBench: Evaluating LLM Safety in Real-World Financial Scenarios
items.abstract: FinSafetyBench is a bilingual (English-Chinese) red-teaming benchmark designed to systematically evaluate the safety and compliance refusal capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) in real-world financial scenarios. Grounded in financial crime cases, it comprises 14 subcategories testing violations across financial crimes and ethics. The paper's contribution is identifying critical vulnerabilities in LLMs, showing stronger susceptibility in Chinese contexts, and demonstrating the limitations of current prompt-level defenses against sophisticated financial manipulation.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the FinSafetyBench pipeline, which consists of extraction and summarization of real-world financial cases, controlled rephrasing with harmfulness verification, selection and integration of public datasets, bilingual alignment, and deduplication with final dataset assembly. The right panel presents an illustrative example of ethical violations. Drawing on real-world cases, FinSafetyBench incorporates more realistic details. Green highlights distinctive features (differences), while red indicates similarities.

items.title: ML-Bench&Guard: Policy-Grounded Multilingual Safety Benchmark and Guardrail for Large Language Models
items.abstract: This paper introduces **ML-Bench**, a novel multilingual safety benchmark grounded directly in regional regulations across 14 languages, moving beyond general risk taxonomies. This policy-grounded approach ensures safety evaluations are aligned with specific cultural and legal nuances. Based on this benchmark, the authors develop **ML-Guard**, a Diffusion LLM-based guardrail model capable of multilingual safety judgment.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the ML-Guard . ML-Guard is trained on ML-Bench . ML-Guard -1.5B performs fast binary safety classification, while ML-Guard -7B supports both safety assessment and policy-conditioned violation checking.

items.title: ReLay: Personalized LLM-Generated Plain-Language Summaries for Better Understanding, but at What Cost?
items.abstract: ReLay introduces a novel dataset of participant-summary pairs to study personalized Plain Language Summaries (PLS) generated by Large Language Models (LLMs). The core method involves evaluating LLM personalization strategies against static summaries using user characteristics, interaction logs, and comprehension outcomes. The contribution is demonstrating that personalization can improve comprehension while providing a benchmark to assess the trade-offs between personalization effectiveness and potential costs.
items.figure_caption: ReLay construction illustration. Of the 397 recruited participants, 50 met eligibility criteria and completed both delivery settings, each involving three scientific abstracts. For the first three abstracts, participants reported their familiarity with terms selected by three medical expert annotators, indicated any additional information needs, read an expert-written PLS, and answered comprehension and evaluation questions curated by the same experts. For the three remaining abstracts, participants conversed with a chatbot, received a personalized PLS, and answered the same expert-selected comprehension and evaluation questions.

items.title: When LLMs Stop Following Steps: A Diagnostic Study of Procedural Execution in Language Models
items.abstract: This paper introduces a diagnostic benchmark to assess whether Large Language Models (LLMs) faithfully execute multi-step procedural instructions, moving beyond simple final-answer accuracy. The study reveals that as the length and complexity of arithmetic algorithms increase, model accuracy significantly degrades, often due to failures like missing steps, premature termination, or hallucinated additions. The core contribution is demonstrating that apparent reasoning capabilities can mask fundamental weaknesses in consistent, step-by-step instruction following.
items.figure_caption: Accuracy of various language models as a function of algorithmic step count (5–95). Performance consistently declines with increasing steps across all models, highlighting growing difficulty in maintaining correct execution over longer procedural sequences despite the simplicity of individual operations.

items.title: Can Coding Agents Reproduce Findings in Computational Materials Science?
items.abstract: This paper introduces **AutoMat**, a new benchmark designed to evaluate the capability of LLM-based coding agents to reproduce findings in computational materials science. AutoMat tests agents on three core challenges: recovering underspecified procedures, navigating specialized toolchains, and validating scientific claims based on the resulting evidence. The contribution lies in creating a domain-specific, expert-curated test suite to assess if general coding prowess translates to complex, end-to-end scientific reproducibility.
items.figure_caption: Overview of AutoMat . Claims from domain experts are packaged into runnable tasks and executed by reproduction agents in an HPC environment. A separate evaluator agent then inspects the resulting trace and artifacts to assign a reproducibility judgment.

items.title: Empowering Heterogeneous Graph Foundation Models via Decoupled Relation Alignment
items.abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of applying Graph Foundation Models to multi-domain heterogeneous graphs by introducing **Decoupled Relation Subspace Alignment (DRSA)**. DRSA overcomes feature shifts and relation gaps by decoupling feature semantics from relation structures. It employs a dual-relation subspace projection to explicitly coordinate cross-type interactions within a shared low-rank space, preventing the "Type Collapse" and "Relation Confusion" seen in previous alignment methods.
items.figure_caption: Multi-domain heterogeneous graph foundation models exhibit significantly different negative transfer behaviors from the perspectives of meta-path-based homogeneous graphs and raw heterogeneous relation graphs.

items.title: Jailbreaking Vision-Language Models Through the Visual Modality
items.abstract: This paper introduces four novel jailbreaking attacks that exploit the visual modality of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to bypass safety alignment, an underexplored attack surface. The core method involves encoding harmful intent through visual means, such as visual symbol sequences or object substitution. The key contribution is demonstrating a significant cross-modality alignment gap, showing that text-based safety training fails to generalize to visually conveyed harmful instructions.
items.figure_caption: Four visual jailbreak attacks exploiting the vision modality of VLMs. The visual input provided to the VLM is demarcated by the red boundary ( ), while the text beneath serves as the attack prompt.

items.title: Learn where to Click from Yourself: On-Policy Self-Distillation for GUI Grounding
items.abstract: This paper introduces GUI-SD, the first On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) framework specifically designed for GUI grounding. It addresses the limitations of traditional reinforcement learning by generating dense, token-level supervision from a single rollout. The core method uses a visually enriched privileged context for the teacher and employs entropy-guided distillation to adaptively focus learning on more significant tokens.
items.figure_caption: (a) GRPO requires expensive multiple rollouts and produces zero reward on hard samples. (b) Naive OPSD forwards the policy twice and distills via reverse KL between student and teacher logits with uniform per-token weight w = 1.0 w=1.0 , yet suffers from distillation-to-SFT collapse and indiscriminate optimization. (c) Ours addresses both issues via visual privileged guidance and entropy-guided optimization.

items.title: Make Your LVLM KV Cache More Lightweight
items.abstract: LightKV addresses the significant GPU memory overhead of KV caches in LVLMs by exploiting redundancy among vision tokens during the prefill stage. It uses prompt-aware, cross-modality message passing to aggregate informative content across vision tokens and progressively compress them. This results in halving the vision-token KV cache size while retaining only 55% of the original vision tokens, significantly improving memory efficiency.
items.figure_caption: Breakdown of memory consumption in LLaVA models during prefill shows the substantial reduction in KV cache usage with LightKV. As LLaVA-NeXT uses approximately 4 × 4\( \times \) the vision tokens as LLaVA-v1.5, there is a sharp increase in memory consumption.

items.title: Space Network of Experts: Architecture and Expert Placement
items.abstract: This paper introduces the **Space Network of Experts (Space-XNet)** framework to efficiently deploy large Language Models (LLMs) on resource-constrained satellite networks for space-based AI execution. The core method involves a **two-level expert placement strategy** that partitions and maps Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model components across the satellite network topology. This addresses the challenge of reconciling LLM architecture with limited onboard resources to ensure low-latency token generation in space.
items.figure_caption: Satellite constellation with time-varying network topologies.

items.title: Let's Buy Spirit Air
items.summary: The provided URL appears to be a speculative or non-official website related to the concept of buying Spirit Airlines, rather than a traditional news report.

items.title: For thirty years I programmed with Phish on, every day
items.summary: A programmer shares a personal reflection on how listening to the band Phish daily for thirty years influenced their programming workflow and state of flow.

items.title: LLMs Are Not a Higher Level of Abstraction
items.summary: The core argument is that Large Language Models (LLMs) do not represent a fundamentally new or higher level of abstraction in computation, but rather are sophisticated pattern-matching systems operating within existing frameworks.

items.description: 🌊 The leading agent orchestration platform for Claude. Deploy intelligent multi-agent swarms, coordinate autonomous workflows, and build conversational AI systems. Features enterprise-grade architecture, self-learning swarm intelligence, RAG integration, and native Claude Code / Codex Integration
items.summary: ruflo is a leading agent orchestration platform for Claude, enabling the deployment of intelligent, self-learning multi-agent swarms for autonomous workflows and conversational AI, featuring enterprise architecture and RAG integration.

items.description: TradingAgents: Multi-Agents LLM Financial Trading Framework
items.summary: TradingAgents is a multi-agent LLM framework designed for developing and testing sophisticated financial trading strategies.

items.description: Claude Agent SDK with a web browsing tool
items.summary: The browserbase/skills repository provides a Claude Agent SDK featuring a built-in web browsing tool, enabling agents to interact with and gather information from the internet.

items.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
items.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that allows users to interact with and utilize DeepSeek models directly within their command-line interface.

items.description: 🕵️‍♂️ Collect a dossier on a person by username from 3000+ sites
items.summary: Maigret is a tool that collects a dossier on a person by querying over 3000 websites using a provided username.

qBittorrent is a free and open-source BitTorrent client that offers a cross-platform, feature-rich application for downloading and managing torrents, often noted for its clean interface and integration of a search engine.

items.description: A MCP for Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Windsurf / Cursor to build n8n workflows for you
items.summary: This repository provides an n8n workflow builder, acting as an MCP (Macro Control Panel) interface, specifically designed to integrate with AI coding assistants like Claude Desktop and Cursor for automated workflow creation.
jcode provides a flexible harness for building and running coding agents, facilitating the development and testing of AI-powered code generation and manipulation tools.

items.description: A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.
items.summary: This repository provides a complete AI agency framework with specialized, personality-driven agents—ranging from frontend wizards to reality checkers—each equipped with defined processes and proven deliverables.

papers.title: Silicon Showdown: Performance, Efficiency, and Ecosystem Barriers in Consumer-Grade LLM Inference
papers.abstract: This paper systematically analyzes the performance and efficiency trade-offs for running large LLMs (70B+ parameters) on consumer Nvidia and Apple Silicon hardware. For Nvidia, it identifies a "Backend Dichotomy" where the new NVFP4 format boosts throughput significantly but imposes runtime constraints affecting latency. The study also characterizes the "VRAM Wall," forcing users to choose between fitting large models via aggressive quantization or sacrificing model intelligence.

papers.title: To Call or Not to Call: A Framework to Assess and Optimize LLM Tool Calling
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a decision-making framework to assess and optimize when Large Language Models (LLMs) should utilize external tools, focusing specifically on web search. The framework evaluates tool-use decisions based on necessity, utility, and affordability, using both normative (optimal allocation) and descriptive (observed behavior) perspectives. This approach allows for a principled analysis of LLMs' self-awareness regarding the true benefit of external information retrieval.
papers.figure_caption: Given input x x , the model ℳ \( \mathcal{M} \) decides π ​ ( x ) ∈ { 0 , 1 } \( \pi \)(x)\( \in \)\{0,1\} to call a tool (response r r ) or not, producing y = ℳ ​ ( x , r ) y=\( \mathcal{M} \)(x,r) or y = ℳ ​ ( x ) y=\( \mathcal{M} \)(x) . We compare NO TOOL, ALWAYS TOOL, and SELF-DECISION, and evaluate decisions via need (requires help), utility (performance gain), and affordability (cost vs. gain), distinguishing perceived vs. true quantities.

papers.title: Evaluating the Architectural Reasoning Capabilities of LLM Provers via the Obfuscated Natural Number Game
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the Obfuscated Natural Number Game to evaluate LLMs' **Architectural Reasoning**—the ability to prove theorems using only local axioms in an unfamiliar domain. By renaming identifiers in the Lean 4 formalization, they created a zero-knowledge benchmark. The study finds that while obfuscation universally increases inference time, general models degrade significantly, whereas specialized reasoning models maintain consistent accuracy.
papers.figure_caption: LLM performance metrics across varying noise levels \( \lambda \) . The plots illustrate Correct Rate (%) and Average Time (s) for GPT-4o, Claude-Sonnet-4.5, DeepSeek-R1, GPT-5, and DeepSeek-Prover-V2. Error bars represent standard deviation over 5 independent runs.

papers.title: RunAgent: Interpreting Natural-Language Plans with Constraint-Guided Execution
papers.abstract: RunAgent is a multi-agent platform designed to reliably execute natural-language plans by enforcing determinism through an agentic language with explicit control structures. Its core contribution is integrating natural language understanding with constraint-guided execution, autonomously deriving and validating step-specific constraints. This allows RunAgent to dynamically select appropriate actions (reasoning, tool use, or code execution) while ensuring correctness through verification and error correction.
papers.figure_caption: An overview of RunAgent, highlighting its three main modules.

papers.title: Stable-GFlowNet: Toward Diverse and Robust LLM Red-Teaming via Contrastive Trajectory Balance
papers.abstract: Stable-GFN (S-GFN) addresses the instability and mode collapse issues in using Generative Flow Networks (GFNs) for LLM red-teaming. The core method involves eliminating the partition function ($Z$) estimation via pairwise comparisons and employing robust masking against noisy rewards. This results in more stable training, leading to superior attack performance and greater diversity in identifying LLM vulnerabilities.

papers.title: AGoQ: Activation and Gradient Quantization for Memory-Efficient Distributed Training of LLMs
papers.abstract: AGoQ introduces a novel quantization method for memory-efficient LLM distributed training by addressing the limitations of standard 4-bit activations and 8-bit gradients. It employs a layer-aware activation quantization to achieve near 4-bit storage and a precision-preserving 8-bit gradient quantization with optimized All-Reduce communication. This approach significantly reduces memory usage (up to 52%) and improves training speed (up to 1.34$\times$) without sacrificing accuracy.
papers.figure_caption: An example of Interleaved 1F1B PP with four stages and each mini-batch divided into eight micro-batches.

papers.title: Beyond Benchmarks: MathArena as an Evaluation Platform for Mathematics with LLMs
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **MathArena** as a continuously maintained evaluation platform designed to overcome the limitations of static benchmarks for assessing LLMs in mathematics. It significantly broadens the original scope to include diverse tasks like proof-based competitions, research-level problems, and formal proof generation in Lean. The core contribution is providing a comprehensive, regularly updated system for reliable comparison and tracking of LLM mathematical reasoning capabilities.

papers.title: FinSafetyBench: Evaluating LLM Safety in Real-World Financial Scenarios
papers.abstract: FinSafetyBench is a bilingual (English-Chinese) red-teaming benchmark designed to systematically evaluate the safety and compliance refusal capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) in real-world financial scenarios. Grounded in financial crime cases, it comprises 14 subcategories testing violations across financial crimes and ethics. The paper's contribution is identifying critical vulnerabilities in LLMs, showing stronger susceptibility in Chinese contexts, and demonstrating the limitations of current prompt-level defenses against sophisticated financial manipulation.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the FinSafetyBench pipeline, which consists of extraction and summarization of real-world financial cases, controlled rephrasing with harmfulness verification, selection and integration of public datasets, bilingual alignment, and deduplication with final dataset assembly. The right panel presents an illustrative example of ethical violations. Drawing on real-world cases, FinSafetyBench incorporates more realistic details. Green highlights distinctive features (differences), while red indicates similarities.

papers.title: ML-Bench&Guard: Policy-Grounded Multilingual Safety Benchmark and Guardrail for Large Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **ML-Bench**, a novel multilingual safety benchmark grounded directly in regional regulations across 14 languages, moving beyond general risk taxonomies. This policy-grounded approach ensures safety evaluations are aligned with specific cultural and legal nuances. Based on this benchmark, the authors develop **ML-Guard**, a Diffusion LLM-based guardrail model capable of multilingual safety judgment.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the ML-Guard . ML-Guard is trained on ML-Bench . ML-Guard -1.5B performs fast binary safety classification, while ML-Guard -7B supports both safety assessment and policy-conditioned violation checking.

papers.title: ReLay: Personalized LLM-Generated Plain-Language Summaries for Better Understanding, but at What Cost?
papers.abstract: ReLay introduces a novel dataset of participant-summary pairs to study personalized Plain Language Summaries (PLS) generated by Large Language Models (LLMs). The core method involves evaluating LLM personalization strategies against static summaries using user characteristics, interaction logs, and comprehension outcomes. The contribution is demonstrating that personalization can improve comprehension while providing a benchmark to assess the trade-offs between personalization effectiveness and potential costs.
papers.figure_caption: ReLay construction illustration. Of the 397 recruited participants, 50 met eligibility criteria and completed both delivery settings, each involving three scientific abstracts. For the first three abstracts, participants reported their familiarity with terms selected by three medical expert annotators, indicated any additional information needs, read an expert-written PLS, and answered comprehension and evaluation questions curated by the same experts. For the three remaining abstracts, participants conversed with a chatbot, received a personalized PLS, and answered the same expert-selected comprehension and evaluation questions.

papers.title: When LLMs Stop Following Steps: A Diagnostic Study of Procedural Execution in Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a diagnostic benchmark to assess whether Large Language Models (LLMs) faithfully execute multi-step procedural instructions, moving beyond simple final-answer accuracy. The study reveals that as the length and complexity of arithmetic algorithms increase, model accuracy significantly degrades, often due to failures like missing steps, premature termination, or hallucinated additions. The core contribution is demonstrating that apparent reasoning capabilities can mask fundamental weaknesses in consistent, step-by-step instruction following.
papers.figure_caption: Accuracy of various language models as a function of algorithmic step count (5–95). Performance consistently declines with increasing steps across all models, highlighting growing difficulty in maintaining correct execution over longer procedural sequences despite the simplicity of individual operations.

papers.title: Can Coding Agents Reproduce Findings in Computational Materials Science?
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **AutoMat**, a new benchmark designed to evaluate the capability of LLM-based coding agents to reproduce findings in computational materials science. AutoMat tests agents on three core challenges: recovering underspecified procedures, navigating specialized toolchains, and validating scientific claims based on the resulting evidence. The contribution lies in creating a domain-specific, expert-curated test suite to assess if general coding prowess translates to complex, end-to-end scientific reproducibility.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of AutoMat . Claims from domain experts are packaged into runnable tasks and executed by reproduction agents in an HPC environment. A separate evaluator agent then inspects the resulting trace and artifacts to assign a reproducibility judgment.

papers.title: Empowering Heterogeneous Graph Foundation Models via Decoupled Relation Alignment
papers.abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of applying Graph Foundation Models to multi-domain heterogeneous graphs by introducing **Decoupled Relation Subspace Alignment (DRSA)**. DRSA overcomes feature shifts and relation gaps by decoupling feature semantics from relation structures. It employs a dual-relation subspace projection to explicitly coordinate cross-type interactions within a shared low-rank space, preventing the "Type Collapse" and "Relation Confusion" seen in previous alignment methods.
papers.figure_caption: Multi-domain heterogeneous graph foundation models exhibit significantly different negative transfer behaviors from the perspectives of meta-path-based homogeneous graphs and raw heterogeneous relation graphs.

papers.title: Jailbreaking Vision-Language Models Through the Visual Modality
papers.abstract: This paper introduces four novel jailbreaking attacks that exploit the visual modality of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to bypass safety alignment, an underexplored attack surface. The core method involves encoding harmful intent through visual means, such as visual symbol sequences or object substitution. The key contribution is demonstrating a significant cross-modality alignment gap, showing that text-based safety training fails to generalize to visually conveyed harmful instructions.
papers.figure_caption: Four visual jailbreak attacks exploiting the vision modality of VLMs. The visual input provided to the VLM is demarcated by the red boundary ( ), while the text beneath serves as the attack prompt.

papers.title: Learn where to Click from Yourself: On-Policy Self-Distillation for GUI Grounding
papers.abstract: This paper introduces GUI-SD, the first On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) framework specifically designed for GUI grounding. It addresses the limitations of traditional reinforcement learning by generating dense, token-level supervision from a single rollout. The core method uses a visually enriched privileged context for the teacher and employs entropy-guided distillation to adaptively focus learning on more significant tokens.
papers.figure_caption: (a) GRPO requires expensive multiple rollouts and produces zero reward on hard samples. (b) Naive OPSD forwards the policy twice and distills via reverse KL between student and teacher logits with uniform per-token weight w = 1.0 w=1.0 , yet suffers from distillation-to-SFT collapse and indiscriminate optimization. (c) Ours addresses both issues via visual privileged guidance and entropy-guided optimization.

papers.title: Make Your LVLM KV Cache More Lightweight
papers.abstract: LightKV addresses the significant GPU memory overhead of KV caches in LVLMs by exploiting redundancy among vision tokens during the prefill stage. It uses prompt-aware, cross-modality message passing to aggregate informative content across vision tokens and progressively compress them. This results in halving the vision-token KV cache size while retaining only 55% of the original vision tokens, significantly improving memory efficiency.
papers.figure_caption: Breakdown of memory consumption in LLaVA models during prefill shows the substantial reduction in KV cache usage with LightKV. As LLaVA-NeXT uses approximately 4 × 4\( \times \) the vision tokens as LLaVA-v1.5, there is a sharp increase in memory consumption.

papers.title: Space Network of Experts: Architecture and Expert Placement
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the **Space Network of Experts (Space-XNet)** framework to efficiently deploy large Language Models (LLMs) on resource-constrained satellite networks for space-based AI execution. The core method involves a **two-level expert placement strategy** that partitions and maps Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model components across the satellite network topology. This addresses the challenge of reconciling LLM architecture with limited onboard resources to ensure low-latency token generation in space.
papers.figure_caption: Satellite constellation with time-varying network topologies.

hacker_news.title: Let's Buy Spirit Air
hacker_news.summary: The provided URL appears to be a speculative or non-official website related to the concept of buying Spirit Airlines, rather than a traditional news report.

hacker_news.title: For thirty years I programmed with Phish on, every day
hacker_news.summary: A programmer shares a personal reflection on how listening to the band Phish daily for thirty years influenced their programming workflow and state of flow.

hacker_news.title: LLMs Are Not a Higher Level of Abstraction
hacker_news.summary: The core argument is that Large Language Models (LLMs) do not represent a fundamentally new or higher level of abstraction in computation, but rather are sophisticated pattern-matching systems operating within existing frameworks.

github_trending.description: 🌊 The leading agent orchestration platform for Claude. Deploy intelligent multi-agent swarms, coordinate autonomous workflows, and build conversational AI systems. Features enterprise-grade architecture, self-learning swarm intelligence, RAG integration, and native Claude Code / Codex Integration
github_trending.summary: ruflo is a leading agent orchestration platform for Claude, enabling the deployment of intelligent, self-learning multi-agent swarms for autonomous workflows and conversational AI, featuring enterprise architecture and RAG integration.

github_trending.description: TradingAgents: Multi-Agents LLM Financial Trading Framework
github_trending.summary: TradingAgents is a multi-agent LLM framework designed for developing and testing sophisticated financial trading strategies.

github_trending.description: Claude Agent SDK with a web browsing tool
github_trending.summary: The browserbase/skills repository provides a Claude Agent SDK featuring a built-in web browsing tool, enabling agents to interact with and gather information from the internet.

github_trending.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
github_trending.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that allows users to interact with and utilize DeepSeek models directly within their command-line interface.

github_trending.description: 🕵️‍♂️ Collect a dossier on a person by username from 3000+ sites
github_trending.summary: Maigret is a tool that collects a dossier on a person by querying over 3000 websites using a provided username.

qBittorrent is a free and open-source BitTorrent client that offers a cross-platform, feature-rich application for downloading and managing torrents, often noted for its clean interface and integration of a search engine.

github_trending.description: A MCP for Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Windsurf / Cursor to build n8n workflows for you
github_trending.summary: This repository provides an n8n workflow builder, acting as an MCP (Macro Control Panel) interface, specifically designed to integrate with AI coding assistants like Claude Desktop and Cursor for automated workflow creation.
jcode provides a flexible harness for building and running coding agents, facilitating the development and testing of AI-powered code generation and manipulation tools.

github_trending.description: A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a complete AI agency framework with specialized, personality-driven agents—ranging from frontend wizards to reality checkers—each equipped with defined processes and proven deliverables.
